message:
Fix argless shortest_path(G) to yield a generator (br-1uoos)

NetworkX's shortest_path(G, source=None, target=None) dispatches to
all_pairs_shortest_path / all_pairs_dijkstra / all_pairs_bellman_ford,
all of which return a generator yielding (source, paths_dict) pairs. The
Rust-backed _raw_shortest_path materialises the full dict-of-dicts up
front, so fnx.shortest_path(G) was handing callers a plain dict instead
of a generator. Anything that did `next(...)`, `list(...)` of pairs,
or fed the result into APIs expecting the all_pairs contract broke on
fnx but worked on upstream nx.

This wraps the _raw_shortest_path call in the Python-level shortest_path
dispatcher so that the no-endpoint case rehydrates the materialized dict
into `((src, paths) for src, paths in result.items())`, matching the
upstream contract while preserving the Rust fast path. shortest_path_length
already returned a generator, so this brings shortest_path into line.

Adds a regression test test_shortest_path_argless_returns_generator that
asserts GeneratorType, verifies the materialised mapping still has the
correct paths (path_graph(4) -> 0..3), and confirms next() works on it
the way it does on nx.

Also syncs .beads/issues.jsonl to flush the br-1uoos tracking bead
(status in_progress) so the audit trail stays aligned with the fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1837,7 +1837,13 @@ def shortest_path(G, source=None, target=None, weight=None, method="dijkstra"):
                 weight=weight,
                 method=method,
             )
-    return _raw_shortest_path(G, source=source, target=target, weight=weight, method=method)
+    result = _raw_shortest_path(G, source=source, target=target, weight=weight, method=method)
+    if source is None and target is None and isinstance(result, dict):
+        # networkx returns a generator of (source, paths_dict) pairs when both
+        # endpoints are omitted (dispatches to all_pairs_*). The Rust native
+        # path materializes the full dict-of-dicts; convert to match contract.
+        return ((src, paths) for src, paths in result.items())
+    return result
 
 
 def shortest_path_length(G, source=None, target=None, weight=None, method="dijkstra"):

@@ -729,6 +729,25 @@ def test_single_source_shortest_path_length(self):
         assert lengths[0] == 0
         assert lengths[3] == 3
 
+    def test_shortest_path_argless_returns_generator(self):
+        """Regression (br-1uoos): shortest_path(G) with no source/target
+        must return a generator of (source, paths_dict) pairs to match
+        networkx's all_pairs contract, not a materialized dict-of-dicts.
+        """
+        import types
+        G = fnx.path_graph(4)
+        result = fnx.shortest_path(G)
+        assert isinstance(result, types.GeneratorType), (
+            f"expected generator, got {type(result).__name__}"
+        )
+        materialized = dict(result)
+        assert sorted(materialized) == [0, 1, 2, 3]
+        assert materialized[0][3] == [0, 1, 2, 3]
+        # Calling next() on it should work like networkx
+        result2 = fnx.shortest_path(G)
+        first = next(result2)
+        assert isinstance(first, tuple) and len(first) == 2
+
     def test_multi_source_dijkstra(self):
         G = fnx.path_graph(5)
         G.add_edge(0, 1, weight=1.0)
